data.title: Trend Micro targets 6% net sales growth and 25% operating income growth for FY2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Trend Micro Incorporated (OTCPK:TMICY) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Eva Chen emphasized the transformative potential of AI in the cybersecurity landscape, introducing the concept of "proactive cybersecurity" driven by AI democratization. Chen highlighted the company's investment in its AI-based platform, Trend Cybertron, to address emerging threats and optimize security for enterprise and consumer clients.

  • CFO Mahendra Negi reported that Q4 2024 delivered record pre-GAAP organic growth, with net sales increasing 20% year-over-year and pre-GAAP operating income reaching ¥41.1 billion. He noted that without a ¥1.7 billion impact from Digital River's financial issues, pre-GAAP growth would have been 22%.

  • COO Kevin Simzer detailed an enterprise business growth of 24% year-over-year in Q4, supported by the adoption of the Vision One platform. He also noted that recurring revenue across all segments reached $1.7 billion, with large enterprise recurring revenue comprising $1.1 billion, up 8% year-over-year.

  • The company announced a dividend of ¥184 per share for FY2024, significantly higher than the ¥56 per share in FY2023 on an adjusted comparison.

Outlook

  • Management projects a 6% growth in net sales and a 25% growth in operating income for FY2025, with minimal FX impact. They expect growth across all regions, with EMEA and Asia targeted at 10% each.

  • Enterprise subscription business is forecast to grow at 15%, while perpetual renewable offerings are expected to see single-digit growth.

  • Investment in AI and headcount expansion are planned to support future sales growth, with particular focus on enhancing the Vision One platform.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 net sales increased 20% year-over-year, reaching over ¥100 billion. Pre-GAAP profitability improved to 40% from 27% a year ago, marking the highest-ever pre-GAAP organic growth for the company.

  • Operating income for FY2024 was the highest in the company’s history. This improvement was attributed to double-digit growth in the enterprise segment and cost efficiencies achieved through reduced headcount and optimized use of technology.

  • Consumer business faced a ¥1.7 billion impact due to Digital River's financial problems, and management expects an additional ¥20 million to ¥30 million negative impact in FY2025 from the transition to a new vendor.

  • Selling and marketing expenses increased, driven by higher performance-based remuneration tied to pre-GAAP results.

Q&A

  • Hideaki Tanaka, Morgan Stanley: Asked about pre-GAAP metrics and multiyear contracts. COO Kevin Simzer explained that multiyear transactions rose by 5 percentage points to 37% of total deals, supported by changes in sales compensation plans. Simzer noted that this trend is expected to continue as customers seek long-term partnerships with the Vision One platform.

  • Hiroko Sato, Jefferies Securities: Inquired about cost growth and operating income. CFO Mahendra Negi confirmed that cost increases were primarily linked to performance-based bonuses and cloud-related expenses but highlighted efficiency improvements through AI.

  • Unidentified Analyst: Questioned SMB growth through MSP channels. EVP Akihiko Omikawa highlighted a 618% year-over-year increase in Q4 XDR services through MSP partners, with monthly recurring revenue exceeding ¥100 million.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concerns about the financial impact of Digital River’s issues and the conservative FY2025 guidance despite strong Q4 results. Their tone was slightly skeptical but acknowledged the potential for AI-driven growth.

  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing operational efficiencies and the strategic importance of AI and Vision One. COO Kevin Simzer repeatedly highlighted the company's ability to secure large multiyear contracts and expand its platform offerings.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts appeared more focused on potential risks, while management showed increased optimism about AI innovation and enterprise growth.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Net sales growth accelerated from 6% in Q3 to 20% in Q4, driven by enterprise performance and the Vision One platform. Operating margin, however, declined sequentially.

  • Management’s tone shifted to a more optimistic outlook for FY2025, with a stronger emphasis on AI-driven opportunities and proactive cybersecurity.

  • Analysts' focus shifted from SMB and consumer challenges in Q3 to scaling enterprise growth and addressing risks tied to Digital River in Q4.

  • FY2025 guidance indicated a 6% net sales growth projection, compared to the double-digit growth achieved in FY2024, reflecting a cautious outlook.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified a ¥1.7 billion financial impact from Digital River's issues and an expected additional ¥20 million to ¥30 million negative impact in FY2025.

  • Analysts questioned the sustainability of growth given the shift from multiyear to single-year contracts and potential headwinds in the consumer segment.

  • Management emphasized mitigating risks through AI-driven efficiency and a diversified geographic strategy.

Final Takeaway

Trend Micro reported strong Q4 2024 results, with record pre-GAAP organic growth and robust enterprise segment performance fueled by the Vision One platform. The company outlined a cautious yet optimistic FY2025 outlook, targeting 6% net sales growth and 25% operating income growth, backed by AI innovation and strategic investments. Challenges remain in the consumer segment, but management's focus on operational efficiency and expanding enterprise services underscores its commitment to long-term shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Caterpillar added as a new long idea at Hedgeye
data.description:
Caterpillar (NYSE:CAT) was added as a new long idea at Hedgeye.

"Despite Caterpillar’s post-election underperformance, broader indicators signal renewed economic strength, positive order momentum, and key market capital spending growth," Hedgeye analyst Jay Van Sciver wrote in a note on Tuesday.

"With inflation likely to reaccelerate, infrastructure spending expected to grow into the 2026 IIJA funding deadline, and an aging installed base driving replacement demand, we find CAT’s downbeat top line guidance encouraging," Van Sciver added.

Though there may be risks on the margin side for Caterpillar (NYSE:CAT), price-cost indicators suggest the business has moved past an "inflection point," according to Van Sciver.

Shares of Caterpillar (CAT) edged higher by 0.6% on Tuesday. The stock fell ~5% on Jan. 30 after beating Q4 adjusted earnings estimates but reporting flat or lower sales in its three main business lines, as investors were concerned about elevated inventories of Caterpillar machines at dealerships.

data.title: Huntsman targets $15M EBITDA uplift in 2025 from capacity expansions
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Huntsman Corporation (NYSE:HUN) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Peter Huntsman highlighted the company’s strategic cost reduction efforts, emphasizing actions taken since 2022 that have offset over $150 million in inflationary costs. He noted closures of various facilities in Europe and Southeast Asia, alongside expansions in lower-cost hubs such as San José, Costa Rica, and Kraków, Poland. These measures have reduced SG&A expenses by more than 6%.
  • The CEO also pointed to early signs of recovery in MDI pricing and margins, with polymeric MDI prices in China reaching a three-year high and price increases announced in North America. He added, "2025 will be a year wherein we will continue to minimize our cost structure, optimize our asset footprint, and aggressively push for margin expansion."
  • CFO Phil Lister noted that the Geismar splitter is expected to contribute $15 million in additional EBITDA in 2025, contingent on market developments in key end-use industries.

Outlook

  • Management sees 2025 as a transitional year, with capitalizing on market forces being a key focus. They expect gradual recovery in MDI pricing and volume improvements, particularly in North America and China.
  • The CEO expressed optimism regarding early signs of market stabilization, particularly in MDI pricing, but stressed the importance of broader demand recovery to achieve further margin expansion.
  • Incremental capacity in Conroe, Texas, targeting the chip industry, is expected to reach broader customer qualification later in the year, contributing to Performance Products growth.

Financial Results

  • Management emphasized ongoing cost controls and efficiency improvements, which have kept SG&A expenses flat despite inflationary pressures over the past two years.
  • The company reported significant savings from restructuring activities, including the $40 million initiative completed earlier and additional actions targeting $50 million in cost reductions by the end of next year.
  • MDI pricing in China has reached a three-year high, supported by disciplined production and improved demand. North American pricing actions are also underway, though management acknowledged variability in timing and effectiveness across customer contracts.

Q&A

  • Patrick Cunningham, Citibank: "Can you help us understand the size and scope of restructuring actions in downstream markets?" CEO Huntsman: "We are calibrating our business around customer needs and consolidating system houses to improve efficiency, particularly in Europe. We’ve also withdrawn from some Southeast Asian markets where margins were not favorable."
  • David Begleiter, Deutsche Bank: "What’s driving Performance Products margin improvement?" CEO Huntsman: "Recovery in the construction sector, growth in agricultural applications, and new capacity in Conroe, Texas, servicing the chip industry."
  • Vincent Andrews, Morgan Stanley: "What are your volume expectations in Europe given recent uncertainty?" CEO Huntsman: "We expect relatively flat volumes, with some recovery compared to last year’s outages, but overall conditions remain challenging."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, probing for details on MDI pricing recovery and capacity utilization improvements. Questions reflected interest in the potential for restructuring actions to drive profitability.
  • Management maintained a proactive tone, emphasizing cost reductions, operational efficiency, and strategic expansions. While acknowledging challenges in Europe, the CEO’s remarks signaled confidence, particularly in North America and China.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone appeared more optimistic, bolstered by early signs of pricing recovery and progress in strategic initiatives.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from cautious to cautiously optimistic, reflecting early signs of MDI pricing recovery and progress in cost savings.
  • Strategic focus has evolved toward aggressive margin expansion, with additional capacity in Performance Products and ongoing restructuring actions.
  • Analysts' focus shifted from immediate cost pressures to long-term growth drivers, such as new capacity and pricing recovery.
  • Management confidence in key initiatives, such as the Geismar splitter and recovery in North American construction, appeared stronger than in the previous quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted ongoing challenges in Europe due to high energy costs and regulatory pressures, which continue to weigh on competitiveness.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the sustainability of MDI pricing recovery and the impact of tariffs on trade flows.
  • The company is monitoring geopolitical and economic uncertainties that could affect demand in key markets, particularly North America and China.

Final Takeaway

Huntsman Corporation is positioning itself for a gradual recovery in 2025, with a focus on cost reduction, strategic asset optimization, and margin expansion. Early signs of MDI pricing recovery, coupled with new capacity in Performance Products, provide growth opportunities, though challenges in Europe and broader demand uncertainties remain. Management's proactive approach and cautious optimism signal a potential inflection point for the company’s performance in the coming year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Nike rallies after picking Kim Kardashian as its first-ever partner for a new brand
data.description:
Nike (NYSE:NKE) was the biggest gainer in the Dow Jones Industrial Average on Tuesday after announcing that a new brand, called NikeSKIMS, will launch this spring in the U.S. The collaboration with Kim Kardashian's SKIMS will include training apparel, footwear, and accessories that Nike says combine the company’s sport science with Skims' "obsession" for the female form.

Nike (NYSE:NKE) said it thinks the partnership unlocks an incredible opportunity to disrupt the industry. "We will invite even more athletes into sport and movement with product that makes them feel strong and sexy," highlighted Heidi O'Neill

Notably, the development marks the first time Nike (NKE) has partnered with an outside company to launch a new brand.

Analysts have weighed in favorably on the upside for Nike (NKE), especially for the long term, on the view the athletic apparel giant can add market share in a growing category.

Jefferies analyst Randal Konik said the firm views the NikeSKIMS partnership favorably because it is a promising step forward in the fitness and activewear market. The deal is seen leveraging the strengths of Nike's (NKE) innovation and SKIMS' inclusivity.

"Although we maintain our Hold rating due to near-term headwinds, we see limited downside for NKE shares at current levels. In our view, the long-term potential for growth and market disruption remains compelling, making this collaboration a noteworthy development in the industry," wrote Konik.

Shares of Nike (NKE) ripped a 4.5% gain in afternoon trading and are now in positive territory for 2025.

data.title: Valmont jumps to all-time highs after including some tariff effects with 2025 guidance
data.description:
Valmont Industries (NYSE:VMI) +15.3% in Tuesday's trading to a new all-time intraday high of $372.10 after the company beat estimates for Q4 adjusted earnings, included the effects of higher tariffs in its guidance for further growth in 2025, raised its quarterly dividend, and announced a new buyback program.

Q4 net profit jumped to $77.7M, or $3.84/share, from $28.6M, or $1.38/share, for the year-earlier quarter, and revenues rose 2% Y/Y to $1.04B, slightly above expectations.

The company also hiked its quarterly dividend by 13% to $0.68/share and approved the repurchase of $700M in company shares, reflecting expectations of significant cash flow generation in the coming years, CFO Thomas Liguori said.

For 2025, Valmont (NYSE:VMI) guided for full-year earnings of $17.20-$18.80/share and sales $4B-$4.2B, in line with analyst consensus forecast for $17.92 in EPS and $4.15B in sales.

Valmont (VMI) said its current guidance includes the direct effects from increased tariffs on Chinese goods, as well as steel and aluminum imports, but it left behind the timing and effects of additional U.S. tariffs on all goods imports from Canada and Mexico, as well as retaliatory actions by both countries.

data.title: Ford eliminates bonuses for half of middle management - report
data.description:
In what the company calls an “effort to incentivize an improvement in employee performance,” but looks more like pre-emptive cost-cutting ahead of changes in U.S. trade policies, Ford (NYSE:F) will reportedly pay annual bonuses to only half its middle management, according to sources cited by Reuters.

"We are focused on driving a high-performance culture that recognizes and rewards employees for their business contributions," a Ford spokesperson said to Reuters, echoing recent remarks from CEO Jim Farley regarding incentives to retain top talent and encourage productivity.

However, executive bonuses are also tied to company performance based on metrics that take into account sales, profitability, and vehicle quality, all of which Ford (NYSE:F) has struggled with over the past year. During the fourth quarter, Reuters reported that bonuses would be cut based on lackluster company performance.

The automaker is also bracing for changes in U.S. trade policy that could create “a lot of cost and a lot of chaos,” Farley told an audience at an investor conference.

“If you look at the tariffs, let's be real honest, long term, a 25% tariff across the Mexico and Canadian border would blow a hole in the US industry that we have never seen," Farley warned.

In the company’s latest quarterly results, Ford (NYSE:F) issued soft guidance for FY25, plagued by a struggling EV division, continued warranty costs, competition from overseas rivals, and expectation for more incentive pricing, all of which continue to weigh on the company’s profitability.

“We think the [cautious guidance] was prudent as there are a lot of external factors,” Farley said in a post-earnings interview on CNBC, noting that the impact from U.S. trade policy on the automotive industry could result in “billions of dollars in losses.”

The news sent Ford (F) shares deeper into the red, currently down as much as 10%, ending a three-day winning streak.

data.title: Intel leads chip stocks higher amid TSM, Broadcom deal speculation
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) led chips stocks higher on Tuesday after reports that Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) and Broadcom (AVGO) could each look to acquire parts of the company.

Shares of Intel jumped about 10% on Tuesday. Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), Nvidia and Marvell Technology (MRVL) rose about 1% each. However, TSM and Broadcom were not faring well, having dipped nearly 1% and about 2%, respectively.

Super Micro Computer's (SMCI) stock soared about 12% ahead of its regulatory filing deadline. The company is expected to file its 2024 annual report along with its quarterly reports for the September 2024 and December 2024 periods by the Feb. 25 deadline.

Meanwhile, chip equipment makers, Lam Research (LRCX) climbed 4%, Applied Materials (AMAT) and KLA Corp. (KLAC), each rose about 1%, but ASML (ASML) fell around 1%.

Other semiconductor stocks: Micron Technology (MU) 6%, GlobalFoundries (GFS) 4%, Lattice Semiconductor (LSCC) 6%, Analog Devices (ADI) 2%, Texas Instruments (TXN) +2%.

data.title: Vulcan Materials projects $2.35B-$2.55B adjusted EBITDA for 2025, driven by acquisitions and public construction
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Vulcan Materials Company (NYSE:VMC) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Tom Hill highlighted that 2024 was a year of successful execution, with the company improving aggregates cash gross profit per ton by 12% and deploying over $2 billion towards aggregates-led acquisitions. These acquisitions expanded Vulcan’s presence in new growth markets and strengthened its position in key revenue states.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 2024 reached $550 million, a 16% year-over-year increase. Adjusted EBITDA margin improved for the eighth consecutive quarter.
  • Aggregates freight-adjusted prices increased 11% for the quarter. However, aggregate shipments declined by 3% year-over-year due to mixed geographical performance and a contraction in private construction activity.
  • CFO Mary Andrews Carlisle noted that the company deployed $2.3 billion for acquisitions in 2024 while maintaining a net debt-to-adjusted EBITDA leverage ratio of 2.3x. She emphasized that the acquisitions are expected to contribute $150 million of adjusted EBITDA in 2025.
  • Carlisle reported that SAG expenses were 2% lower than the previous year, while return on invested capital stood at 16.2%.

Outlook

  • Vulcan forecasts adjusted EBITDA between $2.35 billion and $2.55 billion in 2025. Freight-adjusted aggregates prices are expected to grow by 5%-7%, with inflationary cost pressures moderating.
  • Aggregate shipments are projected to increase 3%-5% in 2025, bolstered by acquisitions and stable demand in legacy markets.
  • Public construction activity is expected to drive growth, supported by $45 billion in new state and local funding initiatives. The company anticipates growth in single-family housing starts but expects multifamily housing and private non-residential construction to remain weaker in 2025.
  • Capital expenditures for 2025 are projected between $750 million and $800 million, including $125 million for three major plant rebuild projects.

Financial Results

  • Adjusted EPS for Q4 2024 was not explicitly disclosed in the transcript, so no comparison to analysts' estimates is provided.
  • Aggregate cash gross profit per ton increased 16% to $11.50 in Q4 2024. Freight-adjusted unit cash costs rose by 5% year-over-year, reflecting improved operational efficiencies.
  • The asphalt and concrete segments contributed approximately $360 million in cash gross profit for 2024, with 75% of growth driven by acquisitions.

Q&A

  • Trey Grooms, Stephens, inquired about aggregates pricing momentum. CEO Hill confirmed consistent pricing improvements and guidance of 5%-7% growth for 2025, noting that acquisitions negatively impacted pricing by over 100 basis points.
  • Steven Fisher, UBS, asked about organic volumes and the cadence of non-residential construction trends. Hill stated that public construction growth would offset private sector weaknesses, with easier volume comparisons expected in the latter half of 2025.
  • Kathryn Thompson, Thompson Research Group, questioned gross margin performance and future outlook. Hill attributed Q4 gross margin improvements to better weather, higher volumes, and operational efficiencies.
  • Angel Castillo, Morgan Stanley, asked about private non-residential construction recovery. Hill indicated data centers and warehouses as bright spots, with broader stabilization expected by mid-2025.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed cautious optimism, focusing on volume dynamics, pricing discipline, and acquisition integration. Repeated questions on private non-residential construction reflected lingering uncertainties about demand recovery.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing strong execution, pricing momentum, and operational improvements. CEO Hill frequently highlighted the company’s resilience and ability to navigate challenges.
  • Compared to Q3 2024, the tone in Q4 was more confident, especially regarding pricing and cost management, though concerns about private sector demand persisted.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Adjusted EBITDA margin improved for the eighth consecutive quarter in Q4 2024, compared to Q3 where weather disruptions heavily impacted volumes.
  • Q4 saw stronger pricing momentum with an 11% increase in freight-adjusted prices, consistent with full-year results. Q3 focused on navigating extreme weather events and lower aggregate shipments.
  • Strategic priorities shifted to integrating acquisitions and leveraging public construction growth, compared to Q3’s emphasis on weather recovery and cost management.
  • Management tone in Q4 was more forward-looking with a focus on 2025 guidance, while Q3 discussions were more reactive to weather-related disruptions.

Risks and Concerns

  • Private non-residential construction remains a headwind, with uncertainties around interest rates and economic conditions potentially delaying recovery.
  • Affordability challenges and elevated interest rates could impact residential construction, especially multifamily housing.
  • Inflationary pressures, though moderating, still pose a risk to cost management.
  • Weather-related disruptions remain an unpredictable but significant factor for aggregate shipment volumes.

Final Takeaway

Vulcan Materials delivered strong Q4 2024 results, driven by pricing momentum and operational efficiencies, despite a mixed volume environment. The company’s $2.35 billion-$2.55 billion adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2025 underscores its confidence in public construction growth and recent acquisitions. While challenges in private construction and inflationary pressures persist, Vulcan remains focused on pricing discipline, cost management, and leveraging its expanded footprint to drive long-term shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Volatility watch: Wingstop earnings could lead to a big share price swing
data.description:
Wingstop (NASDAQ:WING) is being closely watched ahead of its earnings report on February 19 due to a higher than normal level of options trading and short interest on the stock higher than restaurant sector peers.

On Wall Street, BTIG has Wingstop (NASDAQ:WING) lined up as a top pick ahead of the restaurant company's earnings report on February 19. Despite expecting a significant sequential deceleration in same-store sales from Q3, analyst Peter Saleh and his team remain optimistic about Wingstop's (WING) Q4 performance.

The firm expects Wingstop (WING) to see a deceleration from the sizzling 20.9% same-store sales growth in Q3, but easily top the consensus estimate for 10.6% growth. The reintroduction of the $16.99 Boneless Meal Bundle is expected to have provided a tailwind during the quarter.

Looking ahead to 2025, Saleh anticipates Wingstop (WING) to deliver mid-single digit same-store sales guidance, in line with long-term projections. He expects an acceleration in unit growth, potentially reaching the mid-teens, and forecasts 337 net new units, above the high-end estimates for 2024.

Saleh also pointed to supply chain benefits as a key factor in Wingstop's (WING) performance and other factors such as reduced wing cost volatility, decoupling costs from the spot market and capturing 400-600 basis points of benefit on food costs.

Looking further ahead, BTIG views Wingstop as a category-leading restaurant concept with best-in-class unit economics and robust unit development. Strong sales driven by national advertising, menu innovation, and delivery are seen continuing to contribute to the company's success. The firm believes Wingstop (WING) has established competitive advantages in technology, digital ordering, and development.

BTIG has a price target of $370 on Wingstop (WING) based on a 47.5x EV/EBITDA multiple applied to the 2025 adjusted EBITDA estimate of $242.5 million, reflecting a premium to the five-year average due to strong sales and unit growth trends. The average Wall Street price target on Wingstop (WING) is $356.60.

Meanwhile, Seeking Alpha analyst Caffital Research warned that the risk-to-reward profile on Wingstop (WING) is now skewed to the negative side.

Options trading on Wingstop (WING) implies a share price move of more than 10% after the earnings report is released. As a reminder, Wingstop (WING) slid more than 20% after its Q3 earnings report.
